leadership.name: David K. Sinatue, Sr.
leadership.bio: David K. Sinatue, Executive Director of the DKS Incubation Center, provides strategic leadership and oversees the design, implementation, and evaluation of programs that promote sustainable business growth, youth empowerment, and environmental responsibility in alignment with the Incubation Center’s mission. He is responsible for guiding organizational strategy, managing partnerships, ensuring financial sustainability, and driving operational excellence across all initiatives. David holds an MSc in Environmental Governance and a BA in Sociology and Public Administration, combining technical expertise with strong leadership skills to advance the Center’s impact across Liberia.

leadership.name: Ballah Davis
leadership.bio: Ballah Davis, Finance Officer at the DKS Incubation Center, oversees all financial operations, ensuring accurate budgeting, reporting, and effective resource allocation to support program delivery and organizational growth. He is responsible for financial planning, compliance, and safeguarding the Center’s assets. Ballah holds an MBA in Banking and a BSc in Economics, combining financial expertise with strategic oversight to strengthen the Center’s operations and impact.

leadership.name: Gifty Benson Ughweri
leadership.bio: Gifty U. Benson, Program Coordinator at the DKS Incubation Center, manages the planning, implementation, and monitoring of all programs, ensuring they are delivered effectively and achieve intended outcomes. She coordinates participant engagement, facilitates training activities, and supports program evaluation to enhance impact and quality. Gifty holds a BA in Mass Communication, combining strong organizational and communication skills to drive the Center’s programmatic success.

leadership.name: J. Musa Sheriff
leadership.bio: J. Musa Sheriff, Lead Program Associate at the DKS Incubation Center, plays a key role in managing and coordinating program operations. He oversees the planning and execution of training sessions, ensures alignment of activities with organizational objectives, monitors program performance, and supports reporting and impact assessment. He also facilitates stakeholder engagement, mentors junior staff, and contributes to the development of program strategies to enhance efficiency, participant outcomes, and overall impact. J. Musa is a BSc Candidate in Marketing and Supply Chain Management, combining academic knowledge with practical skills to strengthen program implementation and operational excellence.

leadership.name: M. Abraham Kortu
leadership.bio: M. Abraham Kortu, Recruitment and Outreach Coordinator at the DKS Incubation Center, leads the planning, implementation, and evaluation of participant recruitment and outreach strategies. He develops targeted engagement campaigns, builds partnerships with community stakeholders, and ensures a diverse and qualified pool of participants for programs. He also monitors recruitment metrics, evaluates outreach effectiveness, and contributes to strategic planning for community engagement and program growth. M. Abraham holds an ASc. in Environmental Science and is a BSc Candidate in Environmental Science, combining technical expertise with strong coordination and communication skills to expand the Center’s reach and impact.

leadership.name: Prince T. L. Phillips
leadership.bio: Prince T. L. Phillips, Lead Program Associate for the DKS Incubation Center’s Green Innovation Program (GIP), oversees the design and implementation of initiatives promoting sustainable business solutions, environmental justice, and climate adaptation and mitigation. He coordinates program activities, mentors participants, monitors progress, and ensures alignment of projects with sustainability goals. He also contributes to research, impact assessments, and strategic planning to advance environmentally responsible entrepreneurship. Prince holds an ASc in Environmental Science and is a BSc Candidate in Environmental Science, combining technical expertise with practical skills to drive innovation and sustainability within the Center’s programs.

board.name: Joy Kotlin
board.bio: Executive Director, Founder, Giving Joy Grants
